title: Ethanol precipitation of RNA from small or large volumes
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8kei6","text":"How to precipitate RNA with ethanol and resuspend it! Two separate protocols - one for small and one for large volumes. The procedure for large volume precipitation is similar, but begins with an initial precipitation and transfers to a small tube for the final steps to avoid loss in the large tube. Strongly recommended to move to a smaller tube.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2e14n","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f3sco","text":"Recommended reading: Walker \u0026 Lorsch RNA purification--precipitation methods.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":37,"length":39}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"71DD08232337410397A1A66E6C6588AF","url":"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/24034331"}}}}

steps: Add 1 ul glycoblue per 250 ul sample volume volume here is the volume after adding NaOAc, not the original volume.
Vortex briefly
Add 2 volumes 100% ethanol volume here is the volume after NaOAc, not the original volume. For example, add 1 ml ethanol to 500 ul NaOAc solution.
Vortex briefly

Incubate at -80 degrees for at least 1 hour or in a dry ice ethanol bath for 30 minutes. [PAUSE] RNA is more stable for long periods when stored in ethanol at low temperature than as a pellet. Shorter incubation times are probably ok - glycoblue protocol recommends 30 minutes at -20 C.
Pellet RNA at 16k g for 25 minutes at 4 °C Tip: orient the tubes so the hinge is pointed outwards and the pellet will be easier to identify along the hinge-side of the tube.

Aspirate supernatant carefully without dislodging pellet, first using a P-1000 for most volume and then a P-20 for the end. Using a vacuum aspirator is not recommended as it is easy to aspirate the pellet.
Gently add 500 ul of 70% ethanol at -20 °C the warmer the 70% ethanol is, the more RNA will re-solubilize. Take the 70% out from the -20 right before adding it and then return it to the -20.
Pellet RNA at 16k g for 5 minutes at 4 °C
Aspirate supernatant carefully without dislodging pellet, first using a P-200 for most volume and then a P-20 for the end.
Quick spin to bring down remaining ethanol
Remove as much ethanol as possible without aspirating pellet using a P-20
Dry for 5 minutes at RT and not longer - fully dried pellets are harder to resuspend
Add the desired volume of water or buffer
Scrape the tube along a tube rack to dislodge pellet
Quick spin
Measure concentration using nanodrop or qubit
Add NaOAc to sample to a final concentration of 0.3M This can be accomplished by diluting the sample by at least five volumes with 0.3M NaOAc or adding 3M NaOAc to a final concentration of 0.3M.
Add 1 ul glycoblue per 250 ul sample volume (after adding NaOAc)
Vortex briefly
Add 2 volumes 100% ethanol (1 ml 100% ethanol to 500 ul NaOAc-diluted sample)
Vortex briefly
Incubate at -80 degrees 1 hour to overnight. [PAUSE] RNA is more stable for long periods when stored in ethanol at low temperature than as a pellet.
Centrifuge in a conical tube at > ~5000g, for 25 minutes

Aspirate supernatant carefully with a serological pipet or vacuum aspirator don't worry about getting all the ethanol off - trying to get all the ethanol off will likely remove some pellet as this is a loose pellet
Add 750 ul of 100% ethanol at -20 °C
Resuspend chunks of pellet in the 100% ethanol by pipetting up and down
Transfer ethanol and chunks to a microfuge tube
Spin for 5 minutes at 16k g at 4 °C
go to step #12 to wash and resuspend pellet

Add NaOAc pH 5.2 to sample to a final concentration of 0.3M This can be accomplished by diluting the sample by at least five volumes with 0.3M NaOAc or adding 3M NaOAc to a final concentration of 0.3M. [CRITICAL] Do not exceed 500 ul NaOAc-diluted volume per 1.5 ml tube. For large volumes, see alternate protocol below.
Record the sample volume. If unknown, you can estimate it using the side of the tube or meausure it by pipetting it.
creator: Stephen Floor
